intent: Produce a Comparison report on three frontend frameworks under a fixed development scenario — (F1) Vue.js, (F2) React, (F3) Svelte — across exactly 5 application categories: (AC1) ease of use for beginners, (AC2) component reusability, (AC3) scalability for large projects, (AC4) performance, (AC5) community and ecosystem support. Ground in >= 120 distinct sandbox URLs and cite >= 60 as markdown links. (A) `__SHOPPING__` >= 12 frameworks with URL + features + popularity + learning resources. (B) `__REDDIT__` >= 30 threads from /r/webdev, /r/vuejs, /r/reactjs, /r/svelte etc., classified as {advice, critique, feature_request, project showcase, discussion}. (C) `__WIKIPEDIA__` >= 25 articles, mandatory: React (web framework), Vue.js, Svelte.js, Frontend frameworks, Component-based architecture, Virtual DOM, Reactivity, Composition API — plus >= 15 more. Output a 3 x 5 decision matrix (framework x application category), every cell rated {best / acceptable / poor} with >= 1 shopping URL + >= 1 reddit URL + >= 1 wiki URL as cited evidence. End with a 'when to pick which framework' section: 3 short paragraphs each explaining which project type picks F1/F2/F3, citing >= 5 reddit threads in support. Do NOT output a TOP-10 list — this is comparison, not ranking. Format: every fact is a markdown link `[label](url)`. Sandbox-local URLs only. No fabrication. Begin directly with the comparison report.
author_notes: Deep-tier task generated by gen_task_spec.py from configs/deep_topics/frontend_framework_comparison.yaml. Golden: must=128, pool=410.
